discoveries.headline: The player corpus is 79.6% intermediate — a coaching goldmine hiding in plain sight
discoveries.insight: Of 750 players detected, 79.6% are intermediate. However: skill estimation has only 45% reproducibility across re-analysis runs. Treat as directional, not precise.

pricing.reasoning: Hudl charges $200-2000/month for coaching analytics. At 1/10th for niche sport early stage: $80-$200/month for individual coaches, $500-$1000/month for coaching platforms.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Skill estimates have 45% reproducibility (per verification). The 'intermediate' dominance may reflect prompt bias — Gemini may default to 'intermediate' when uncertain.

discoveries.headline: 84.3% of clips have AI-identifiable brands — 15.700000000000003% have none
discoveries.insight: 161 of 191 highlight clips have at least one brand detected by AI. Note: brand detection has only 20% reproducibility — the same clip may detect different brands on re-analysis.

pricing.reasoning: StatsBomb charges $50K-200K/yr for sports analytics subscriptions. For a niche sport at early stage, brand intelligence could be $500-$2,000/month per brand subscriber. At 10 brand subscribers: $5K-$20K/month total.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Brand detection agreement is only 20%. The '84.3%' figure may not be stable — a re-run could produce a different number. Needs prompt engineering to improve reliability.

discoveries.headline: Kitchen mastery is the #1 predictor of skill level — data aligns with coaching consensus
discoveries.insight: Kitchen mastery by skill: {'advanced intermediate': 7.33, 'intermediate': 6.69, 'advanced_intermediate': 6.5, 'advanced': 7.5}. 'advanced' players avg 7.5/10. Note: these ratings are AI-estimated from video, not ground-truth measurements.

pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Kitchen mastery being correlated with skill is already known by every coach. The novel claim is that AI can detect it from video — which has only 45% reproducibility.

discoveries.headline: Average rally is 16.6 shots — 65.8% go epic (12+)
discoveries.insight: From 187 clips: avg 16.6 shots/rally. 65.8% are 12+ shots. These are highlight clips (non-random), so actual play likely has shorter average rallies.
evidence: Rally stats: min=1, avg=16.6, max=45, n=187

pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Highlight clips are selected for interesting play — rally lengths in this sample likely skew longer than population average. Need sequential (non-highlight) clips to validate.

discoveries.headline: AI classified 191 clips into story arcs — top: 'grind_rally' (160 clips)
discoveries.insight: Story arcs: {'grind_rally': 160, 'athletic_highlight': 9, 'error_highlight': 7, 'pure_fun': 7, 'dominant_performance': 3}. This could power automated highlight reel curation — the AI identifies which clips have narrative value. Note: arc classification has not been separately verified for consistency.

pricing.reasoning: Automated highlight curation saves 10-20 hrs/week of manual review. At $50/hr editor rate: $2K-$4K/month value. Subscription pricing: $500-$1,500/month.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Story arc classification is subjective. Without human labelers verifying, we don't know if 'error_highlight' vs 'comeback' etc. match human judgment. The commercial value depends on accuracy.

discoveries.headline: Most common shot sequence: 'dink → dink' (1494 occurrences)
discoveries.insight: Top sequences across 2845 transitions: ['dink→dink(1494)', 'drive→drive(503)', 'drive→dink(257)', 'dink→drive(225)', 'volley→volley(71)']. Pattern detection from video is novel but shot-by-shot reproducibility is uncertain (shot count agreement: 40%).

pricing.reasoning: Sportradar play-by-play = $500K+/yr for major sports. At 1/100th for pickleball: $5K-$20K/year for real-time shot sequence data.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Shot detection has only 40% reproducibility. Sequences are derived from individual shot detections, so errors compound. Treat as indicative, not statistically rigorous.

discoveries.headline: Top detected brands: JOOLA(405), Nike(51), HEAD(5)
discoveries.insight: Brand frequency across 191 clips: {'JOOLA': 405, 'Nike': 51, 'HEAD': 5, 'On': 5, 'Franklin': 4}. CRITICAL CAVEAT: 97% of clips have no identified venue. If most clips come from Lifetime facilities (a JOOLA partner), JOOLA dominance is a venue artifact, not a market signal.

pricing.reasoning: ShotTracker = $15K/court/yr. Brand tracking at venue level: $200-500/venue/month.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: If clips are from a Lifetime venue, JOOLA is the house paddle brand. Dominance reflects distribution deals, not organic preference. Multi-venue data required to separate signal from noise.

discoveries.headline: Kitchen play yields 1.8% point win rate — but from highlight clips only
discoveries.insight: Of 2089 kitchen shots detected: 1.8% won the point. Caveat: these are highlight clips (selected for exciting play), so kitchen effectiveness in general play may differ. Shot detection consistency: 40%.

pricing.reasoning: Hudl coaching tier: $200-2000/month. Kitchen analytics add-on: $100-$500/month.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Highlight clips select for dramatic moments. Kitchen win rates in ordinary play could be very different. Also: shot position detection (kitchen vs baseline) has not been separately verified.

discoveries.headline: Sponsorship whitespace: Gatorade(125), BODYARMOR(120), Nike(87) — zero detected presence
discoveries.insight: METHODOLOGY: The AI prompt asks Gemini to identify common sports brand categories. These categories (['Gatorade', 'BODYARMOR', 'Nike', 'Yeti', 'Selkirk']) were flagged as ABSENT — meaning zero instances detected across 191 clips. This does NOT mean these brands aren't in pickleball — it means they weren't visible in these specific highlight clips from this venue.
evidence: Method: AI-flagged absence, not confirmed market gap

pricing.reasoning: Novel data product with no direct comparable. Conservative estimate: $300-$1,000/month per brand subscriber for whitespace alerts across venues.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: Absence of brand detection ≠ absence of brand. The AI may miss logos, and these clips are from ~1 venue. A brand like Gatorade could be at 500 other pickleball venues and not appear in this sample.

discoveries.headline: Paddle detection: JOOLA dominates (124/191 clips) — but likely a venue artifact
discoveries.insight: Paddle brands detected: {'JOOLA': 124, 'CRBN': 2, 'Selkirk': 1, 'HEAD': 1, 'Franklin': 1}. JOOLA appears in 124 clips. Lifetime facilities use JOOLA as their house paddle, so this dominance almost certainly reflects the venue's equipment deal, not organic player preference. Multi-venue data needed.

pricing.reasoning: Paddle market intelligence: $500-$2,000/month for equipment manufacturers. At 5 paddle brand subscribers: $2.5K-$10K/month total.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: JOOLA provides house paddles at Lifetime venues. This is not organic market share — it's distribution. Remove Lifetime clips and JOOLA dominance likely disappears.

discoveries.headline: AI-estimated DUPR range: '3.5-4.0' is modal (169/191 clips)
discoveries.insight: METHODOLOGY: Gemini estimates DUPR based on visual cues (shot speed, positioning, decision quality). Distribution: {'3.5-4.0': 169, '3.0-3.5': 13, '4.0-4.5': 3, '4.5-5.0': 1, '2.5-3.0': 1}. CRITICAL: Verification showed 0% agreement on DUPR estimates across re-analysis. This is NOT a reliable predictor in its current form.

pricing.reasoning: Novel product — no direct comparable. If validated against real DUPR data, could command $1K-$5K/month from tournament platforms. Currently: proof-of-concept only.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

discoveries.sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
discoveries.counter_argument: 0% DUPR agreement in verification testing. The model defaults to '3.5-4.0' regardless of actual skill — this is likely prompt bias, not real prediction. Needs ground-truth DUPR data for calibration.

sample_limitations: 97.4% of clips from 'unknown' — heavy single-venue bias
97.4% of clips have no identifiable venue
Sample is non-random: clips selected from Courtana highlight system, not sequential recording
All clips sourced via CDN highlight groups — may over-represent 'interesting' moments vs typical play
79.6% of players detected as 'intermediate' — skill diversity limited

confidence_caps.low_reproducibility: Further reduced for findings with <50% verification agreement

conclusions: Brand detection inconsistent (20%) — needs prompt tuning
Shot counting varies (40%) — expect ±20% variance per clip
Skill estimates drift between runs (45%) — treat as approximate

bias_flags: 97.4% of clips from 'unknown' — heavy single-venue bias

79.6% of players detected as 'intermediate' — skill diversity limited

confidence_caps.single_venue_dominance: Max confidence 80 (>50% of clips from one venue)
confidence_caps.non_random_sample: Max confidence 75 (highlight clips, not random selection)
confidence_caps.small_n_threshold: Max confidence 60 for any finding with N < 20
